title: Buildor Knowledge Base
description: Index of all knowledge files. Claude should read this file at the start of every session to understand what knowledge is available and find relevant context by keyword matching.
instructions: Match your current task against the keywords below to identify which files to read. If no keywords match, scan the descriptions. After any session that produces new learnings, update this map via the /document skill.

files.description: Project directory layout, module boundaries, and file organization conventions
Technology choices, versions, dependencies, and rationale for each
System architecture, data flow, component relationships, design patterns, agent pool, health monitoring, shared skills sync, operation pool
Architecture and design decisions with rationale (why X over Y)
Surprising behaviors, bugs encountered, and non-obvious pitfalls
Established code patterns, conventions, and reusable approaches in this project
OS-specific, tooling, and machine-specific issues and solutions (gitignored)
Buildor event bus system — event types, how to subscribe/emit, rules for adding new events, agent lifecycle events, mailbox events
Current project phase, completed work (Phases 1-8 of skills/agents), in-progress features, and known issues
Personality system (built-in + custom prompt injection) and worktree dependency setup strategies
Buildor self-identity and capability summary — injected at the top of Claude chat sessions so Buildor always knows who it is regardless of which repo it is working in
Authoring guide for Buildor skills — skill.json schema, prompt.md syntax, param types, execution modes, and full demo examples
Design specification for the app-global self-tuning operation scheduler — resource lanes, adaptive concurrency (TCP slow start), three-tier scheduling, permission pipeline integration
Retrospective from first multi-agent orchestrated development cycle — lessons on phased pipelines, review loops, sub-agent spawning, marker syntax, and recommended flow templates
Pool telemetry stream — how to subscribe, read, and diagnose Operation Pool and Mailbox behavior in real-time during development
